summary: Added a company-level Progression Constellation that turns every money-path lane into a clickable level card with derived gate, reward, scout, and active progress signals.

queries: game UI progression map levels unlocks dashboard design pattern
skill tree progression map UX unlock levels dashboard pattern
gamification progress dashboard levels achievements user interface design pattern
pattern_notes: Progress feedback should make current state, goal distance, and rewards visible without turning the product into a toy.
Skill-tree and progression systems scale better when unlock state is data-driven instead of hand-maintained.
The Atlas version uses existing snapshot fields and lane art so future lanes inherit the same ladder automatically.

ui_contract: Every lane renders as a clickable progression card.
Tier state is derived from blockers, pending requests, level, outcomes, progress, evidence, and traces.
Company stats show total levels, custom games, gated lanes, and average lane charge.
Selecting a progression card syncs URL hash, lane list, detail panel, and active progression highlight.
Mobile uses a bounded scroll rail so more lanes can be added without an unbounded first-screen wall.

checks.result: Progression panel visible, 12 lane cards rendered, 4 stat cells rendered, no horizontal document overflow, no console errors, and mobile lane rail bounded at 720px with internal scroll.
Progression panel visible, 12 lane cards rendered in a six-column grid, no horizontal document overflow, and no console errors.
Progression panel visible, 12 lane cards rendered in a six-column grid, no horizontal document overflow, and no console errors.

Clicking the paid_code_bounties progression card updated the hash to #lane=paid_code_bounties&view=overview, activated the matching progression card and lane-list row, and loaded the lane detail panel.
